metrics.narrative: A detailed, iteratively refined ROI/business case has been co-built with Consumer Cellular across at least four dedicated sessions (27 Mar, 6 Apr, 20 Apr 2026). By 20 Apr 2026 the projected 5-year ROI had been revised upward from $17M on $350M of spend to $32M on $660M of spend, with cycle-time and FTE-recapture metrics (4.4 FTE over 5 years) underpinning it. The buyer's own team pressure-tested the assumptions as 'aggressive' (e.g., halving legal-team savings to avoid double counting, 20 Apr 2026), which shows real internal scrutiny rather than a rubber-stamped case.

champion.narrative: Abe Comarow has driven the deal from discovery through implementation planning: scheduling sessions across Legal, AP, and IT, distributing and iterating the business case, and reporting directly to Karson Keogh that 'we are the front runner' (25 Jun 2026 Next Step). He has consistently opened access to new internal stakeholders (Legal on 16 Jun 2026, Finance/AP on 5 Jun 2026, IT/Infosec twice). The one at-risk signal is pace: he has not yet pushed Legal to resolve the ~7-week-old redline backlog or scheduled the SOW deep-dive he himself flagged as needed on 25 Jun 2026.

competition.narrative: Zycus entered as a 'dark horse' against Coupa and SAP Ariba on the introductory call (20 Oct 2025). As of 25 Jun 2026, Abe Comarow told Karson Keogh that Zycus is the front runner but that Coupa and Zip (Zip HQ) are 'still in the running due to dropping their costs significantly,' calling this a 'slight risk.' SAP Ariba has not been named in any evidence since the 20 Oct 2025 shortlisting call, suggesting (though not explicitly confirmed) it has fallen out of the active field while Coupa and Zip remain live threats on price.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain was established clearly on the introductory call (20 Oct 2025): NetSuite's procurement module was called 'unfit for purpose' due to missing contract hierarchy, weak approval flows, and AP challenges, with Consumer Cellular already 18 months into a NetSuite renewal and planning an exit. The Controller (introduced 5 Jun 2026) reinforced this, describing a '3 or 4 year journey' trying to get AP/procurement approvals right. Fragmented tools, manual PR-to-PO processes, and high non-PO invoice volume requiring manual coding were repeatedly cited pain points.

paper_process.narrative: MSA, Order Form and a draft SOW were sent to the relevant stakeholder for review on 20 May 2026 and remain outstanding as of today (9 Jul 2026, ~7 weeks later), backlogged by a departed legal rep per Abe Comarow (25 Jun 2026 Next Step), who estimated 'a few weeks' more. A SOW deep-dive to scope implementation roles/resources/accurate costs was flagged as the next step on 25 Jun 2026 but has not yet been logged as scheduled. The 7 Jul 2026 Implementation Readiness Session confirmed the next contractual step is CC Legal completing SOW/DPA redlines before Zycus can finalize and incorporate changes.

economic_buyer.narrative: The business case was explicitly built 'to be presented to the CFO for funding approval' with a target go-live before the NetSuite contract renews in 2027 (Avoma, 27 Mar 2026). Next Step History shows a CFO business-case readout was targeted for 8 May 2026, with a follow-up on 12 May 2026 noted as 'waiting on response' from Abe on how the readout went. No confirmed outcome of that CFO readout has since been logged in any source, even though the deal has continued to advance through legal and implementation planning, implying tacit approval but leaving direct CFO engagement unconfirmed.

sources: Avoma: Infosec & IT Asset Management demo, 2026-06-05
decision_process.narrative: The buying process is well mapped across parallel workstreams: sourcing/commercial (Abe Comarow), Legal (Bobbie Reyes, Joseph Reiber, Kane Christianson, plus a second CC attorney on the 16 Jun 2026 Legal demo), Accounts Payable/Finance (Heather Williams, Jason Chan, first exposed to the platform 5 Jun 2026), and IT/InfoSec (Colin Taira, plus several IT/Infosec stakeholders across two 'Infosec & IT Asset Management' sessions on 28 May and 5 Jun 2026). A formal Implementation Readiness Session on 7 Jul 2026 pulled in IT, integration and delivery stakeholders together, evidencing a coordinated, multi-thread process moving toward contracting.

decision_criteria.narrative: There is no formal RFP; the buyer confirmed on the introductory call (20 Oct 2025) that Zycus is being evaluated informally alongside two named rivals rather than via a scored RFP process. Evaluation criteria that have surfaced through the sessions include CLM flexibility (avoiding a 'rigid' checkout system referencing a prior tool, 12 Mar 2026), integration ease with NetSuite (repeated across five separate sessions), transparent module/user-based pricing (versus consumption pricing, 5 Jun 2026), and InfoSec/compliance posture (SOC 1/2, ISO 27001, penetration testing, 5 Jun 2026).

engagement.top_event: Deal team to follow up with Marques Johnson (GRC) by June 26 to secure written InfoSec clearance and confirm no outstanding InfoSec blockers. Marques attended InfoSec/ITAM demos May 28 and June 5 but no formal sign-off is documented.

items.detail: Coupa and Zip are pricing aggressively ('dropping their costs significantly which could be a slight risk' per Abe 2026-06-25), creating late-stage competitive pressure. Abe requested updated pricing 'showcasing list cost and CC pricing' to benchmark Zycus against Coupa's discounted offer. If Zycus cannot justify value (ROI, AI differentiation, Netsuite integration ease) vs. Coupa's lower cost, the buyer may reopen price negotiations or flip back to Coupa. Risk is real but manageable — the $32M ROI case is co-built with the buyer and defensible (Colin: 'feels defensible' 2026-04-20), and AI/Merlin differentiation is validated. Mitigation: deliver updated pricing comparison by 2026-07-10, anchoring in ROI and total-cost-of-ownership (SaaS + services + integration) to justify premium vs. Coupa's upfront discount.
items.status: Active — Abe requested updated pricing on 2026-06-25; not yet delivered

items.detail: Consumer Cellular Legal (Bobbie Reyes, Joseph Reiber, Kane) has held the MSA, Order Form, and draft SOW since 2026-05-20 (over 5 weeks) but is backlogged due to a legal rep departure. Abe stated on 2026-06-25 that redlines 'should take a few weeks,' but no progress update has been provided. Passive waiting will push the close into late August or September 2026, giving Coupa/Zip time to re-engage and potentially flip the buyer. Risk is execution / timing, not fit or engagement — Legal attended CLM demos and raised no objections. Mitigation: offer a dedicated legal-support session with Bobbie Reyes and Joseph Reiber by 2026-07-14 to walk through redline questions, provide Zycus standard positions on jurisdiction/T4C/indemnification, and accelerate review; set a target for Legal to complete redlines by ~2026-07-21 (10 business days) so the deal can move to signature by late July / early August 2026.

Coupa and Zip are pricing aggressively ('dropping their costs significantly which could be a slight risk' per Abe 2026-06-25), creating late-stage competitive pressure. Abe requested updated pricing 'showcasing list cost and CC pricing' to benchmark Zycus against Coupa's discounted offer. If Zycus cannot justify value (ROI, AI differentiation, Netsuite integration ease) vs. Coupa's lower cost, the buyer may reopen price negotiations or flip back to Coupa. Risk is real but manageable — the $32M ROI case is co-built with the buyer and defensible (Colin: 'feels defensible' 2026-04-20), and AI/Merlin differentiation is validated. Mitigation: deliver updated pricing comparison by 2026-07-10, anchoring in ROI and total-cost-of-ownership (SaaS + services + integration) to justify premium vs. Coupa's upfront discount.

Consumer Cellular Legal (Bobbie Reyes, Joseph Reiber, Kane) has held the MSA, Order Form, and draft SOW since 2026-05-20 (over 5 weeks) but is backlogged due to a legal rep departure. Abe stated on 2026-06-25 that redlines 'should take a few weeks,' but no progress update has been provided. Passive waiting will push the close into late August or September 2026, giving Coupa/Zip time to re-engage and potentially flip the buyer. Risk is execution / timing, not fit or engagement — Legal attended CLM demos and raised no objections. Mitigation: offer a dedicated legal-support session with Bobbie Reyes and Joseph Reiber by 2026-07-14 to walk through redline questions, provide Zycus standard positions on jurisdiction/T4C/indemnification, and accelerate review; set a target for Legal to complete redlines by ~2026-07-21 (10 business days) so the deal can move to signature by late July / early August 2026.

The close date of 2026-07-31 (24 days out) is not credible — the SOW scoping call to finalize implementation roles/resources/costs has not been scheduled, Legal redlines have been stalled for 5+ weeks, and the SOW must be agreed before the MSA/OF can be signed (per Zycus contracting memo). Remaining steps — SOW scoping, Legal redlines of MSA/OF/SOW, SOW sign-off by the deal owner's manager and AVP Global Delivery, MSA/OF e-signature — cannot realistically compress into 24 days. A 30–60 day slip is likely, pushing close to late August or September 2026. Risk is a Close Date Risk verdict, not a loss — the deal is healthy, engaged, and progressing, but the date is optimistic. Mitigation: finalize SOW scoping this week (by 2026-07-12), accelerate Legal redlines with proactive support (target completion by 2026-07-21), and reset buyer expectation to late August close.

value.detail: Consumer Cellular Legal (Bobbie Reyes, Joseph Reiber, Kane) has held the MSA, Order Form, and draft SOW since 2026-05-20 (over 5 weeks) but is backlogged due to a legal rep departure. Abe stated on 2026-06-25 that redlines 'should take a few weeks,' but no progress update has been provided. Passive waiting will push the close into late August or September 2026, giving Coupa/Zip time to re-engage and potentially flip the buyer. value.detail: The close date of 2026-07-31 (24 days out) is not credible — the SOW scoping call to finalize implementation roles/resources/costs has not been scheduled, Legal redlines have been stalled for 5+ weeks, and the SOW must be agreed before the MSA/OF can be signed (per Zycus contracting memo). Remaining steps — SOW scoping, Legal redlines of MSA/OF/SOW, SOW sign-off by the deal owner's manager and AVP Global Delivery, MSA/OF e-signature — cannot realistically compress into 24 days. A 30–60 day slip is likely, pushing close to late August or September 2026. Risk is a Close Date Risk verdict, not a loss — the deal is healthy, engaged, and progressing, but the date is optimistic. Mitigation: finalize SOW scoping this week (by 2026-07-12), accelerate Legal redlines with proactive support (target completion by 2026-07-21), and reset buyer expectation to late August close.
